articles.title: The Problem With Topuria vs Makhachev
articles.description: This is one of the biggest fights the UFC can make… and somehow, it still doesn’t feel simple. Ilia Topuria and Islam Makhachev keep circling each other — and the more it comes up, the more complicated it gets. Chael breaks down the strange position the UFC finds itself in. Topuria is rising fast, dominating headlines, and calling his shot. Islam is the champion, but the timing, the weight class, and the circumstances don’t quite line up. And then there’s Justin Gaethje — the man who might be getting overlooked… again. From media strategy to matchmaking politics, this isn’t just about who fights who. It’s about how fighters position themselves, when they take risks, and when they chase the biggest possible moment. Because in today’s UFC, the smartest move isn’t always the next fight… it’s the right fight. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #IliaTopuria #IslamMakhachev #JustinGaethje #ChaelSonnen 0:00 What Do You Do With Topuria?0:17 Big Fight… But Not That Big0:49 The UFC’s Weird Moment1:04 Why Everyone Asks About Islam1:31 The Gaethje Problem2:27 Why Gaethje Gets Overlooked3:05 Topuria vs Islam at 170?3:41 The Real Strategy Behind Callouts4:09 Is Everyone Playing It Wrong?5:12 Fight You Can Get vs Headline Fight6:02 Final Take Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

Stake removed from payout. Terms: sportsbook.draftkings.com/promos. Ends 4/26/26 at 11:59 PM ET. Sponsored by DK. Jiří Procházka vs Carlos Ulberg is basically a pick’em fight—and that should surprise you. Chael Sonnen breaks down why the odds are so close and what bettors might be missing. At first glance, many would expect Procházka to be a clear favorite. But the line tells a different story—and it forces a key question: What has to happen for Ulberg to win? The answer likely comes down to one thing: a knockout. Ulberg has the power, and Procházka’s aggressive style leaves openings—but does that really mean he’s easy to finish? Or is that narrative being overblown because of losses to elite strikers like Alex Pereira? Then there’s the mental side—something odds can’t measure. Ulberg prepared for a bigger, more dangerous opponent and mentally stepped into that challenge. That kind of mindset shift can be a major advantage. On the flip side, Procházka’s experience in championship fights and five-round battles gives him an edge when things get tough. So what are you really betting on? If you’re backing Ulberg, you’re probably betting on a knockout. If you’re backing Procházka, you’re betting on experience, durability, and chaos. Either way, this fight is far closer than most people think—and that’s exactly what makes it dangerous. #UFC #JiriProchazka #CarlosUlberg #MMA #ChaelSonnen 0:00 The Odds Surprise 0:08 Not a Big Favorite 0:25 Even Money Explained 0:41 How Ulberg Wins 1:01 Is Jiri “Easy” to KO? 1:31 The Pereira Factor 1:47 Betting Strategy 2:00 The Mental Game 2:23 “Lights Too Bright” Problem 2:46 Ulberg’s Hidden Risk 3:00 Jiri’s Style & Openings 3:20 Proven Chin Examples 3:48 Championship Experience 4:12 The Mental Unknown 4:21 The Pereira Shift 5:17 Why Ulberg Has an Edge 6:15 Chael’s Personal Example 6:55 The Replacement Effect 7:30 Final Betting Question Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: “Khamzat Is Too Humble” DJ And Khamzat Teach Grappling 🤼‍♂️
The UFC Has a Promotion Problem
articles.description: This isn’t just about the fight — it’s about everything that didn’t happen before it. With Alex Pereira out of the picture, Jiri Prochazka vs Carlos Ulberg is now for the light heavyweight title. But the real issue isn’t who’s fighting… It’s why this fight doesn’t feel big. Chael breaks down the missing piece in today’s fight game: promotion. Why didn’t Ulberg call his shot? Why didn’t Prochazka create a narrative? And why does this fight feel like it just… showed up? Because in MMA, the fight starts long before the cage door closes. The best fighters don’t just win — they sell. They create stakes. They build tension. And right now, this main event is missing all of it. From missed callouts to weak narratives, Chael explains why this fight leaves the UFC in the exact same position no matter who wins. Because if you don’t build the moment… there is no moment. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #JiriProchazka #CarlosUlberg #AlexPereira 0:00 “Words Matter” 0:22 Why Promotion Is Everything 1:11 This Fight Exists Because Pereira Left 1:39 The Problem for Prochazka 1:55 Why Ulberg Has the Advantage 2:13 The Promo Ulberg SHOULD Be Cutting 3:12 Prochazka’s Missed Opportunity 4:17 No Story, No Stakes 5:18 What a Real Champion Does 6:12 Final Reality Check Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Josh Hockett’s AWESOME Media Day Speech
articles.description: This Is either genius or disaster. This is one of the riskiest moves you’ll see in the heavyweight division. Josh Hockett is stepping in against Curtis Blaydes — a top-five heavyweight — in just his third fight. But it’s not just the matchup that’s risky. It’s the way he’s doing it. From staying in character during interviews to building a persona before he’s even established, Hockett is attempting something that almost nobody can pull off. And Chael explains why that might be the hardest part of all. At the same time, this fight highlights a bigger shift in the heavyweight division — the return of high-level wrestling. With names like Daniel Cormier, Tyrell Fortune, and even Gable Steveson in the conversation, the landscape is changing fast. So what happens if Hockett wins? He fast-tracks himself to the top. And if he loses… this whole act could collapse overnight. That’s the gamble. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #CurtisBlaydes #Heavyweight #Wrestling #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Hockett vs Blaydes Setup 0:11 Why Staying in Character Is So Hard 0:58 The Interview That Stood Out 1:30 What Happened With the Mic Cut 2:24 Why This Fight Matters 2:53 Wrestling Problem at Heavyweight 3:24 Why This Is a Huge Opportunity 4:16 Wrestling Is Taking Over Again 4:27 Where Gable Steveson Fits 5:09 The Risk of the Character 5:25 Win Big or Look Like a Fool Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Wrong Guys Got the Title Shot
articles.description: This isn’t about hype — it’s about reality. And right now, the UFC light heavyweight title picture doesn’t add up. With the belt vacated after Alex Pereira stepped away, the UFC booked Jiri Prochazka vs Carlos Ulberg for gold. But here’s the issue: this isn’t the fight fans were asking for — and it’s not the fight the division earned. Chael breaks down why this matchup lacks urgency, why Ulberg may have missed his moment by not calling his shot, and why Prochazka’s path back to the belt comes with more questions than answers. More importantly, he asks the question nobody seems to be asking… Where is Magomed Ankalaev? Ankalaev holds the strongest claim in the division, including a win over Pereira, yet he’s not just out of this title fight — he’s barely in the conversation. This isn’t about talent. It’s about timing, narrative, and responsibility. Because in today’s fight game, being great isn’t enough — you have to demand your moment. And right now, this fight feels like two guys who didn’t. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #JiriProchazka #CarlosUlberg #Ankalaev 0:00 Why This Fight Exists 0:50 Vacant Title Problem 1:28 Why Ulberg Dropped the Ball 2:33 This Fight Doesn’t Sell 2:53 Where Is Ankalaev? 3:48 Prochazka’s Real Story 4:49 The Wrong Matchup 5:33 Two Wrong Guys 6:02 Final Take Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: “Strickland Isn’t This Good 😂” Khamzat Reacts To DJ’s Strickland Impression
The Pereira Problem Nobody Solves  #ufc
articles.description: If Alex Pereira gets the win, the UFC has a serious problem—and no obvious answer. In this clip, the aftermath of a Pereira victory is broken down, and it’s anything but simple. With Tom Aspinall potentially waiting in the wings and the ongoing uncertainty surrounding Jon Jones, the heavyweight picture gets complicated fast. Do you move Pereira straight into Aspinall? Do you hold things together in hopes of a Jones return? Or do you trigger interim or undisputed title clauses to force clarity? Each option comes with trade-offs. Pereira vs. Aspinall is fresh, exciting, and solves the immediate problem—but it might close the door on a massive Jon Jones comeback fight. Waiting, on the other hand, risks slowing down momentum for one of the biggest stars in the sport right now. This is where matchmaking becomes strategy. It’s not just about who deserves the shot—it’s about timing, business, and keeping the division moving without losing blockbuster opportunities. If you follow UFC title pictures, heavyweight drama, and fight business decisions, this is a scenario you need to watch closely. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and inside analysis of the fight game. #AlexPereira #UFC #MMA #TomAspinall #JonJones #UFCHeavyweight #FightTalk

articles.title: Jake Paul vs Nate Diaz… In MMA?!
articles.description: Jake Paul in MMA sounds crazy… until you really think about it. Chael Sonnen breaks down why dismissing Jake Paul might be the same mistake people made when he first entered boxing. Back then, nobody believed it. Now? That story looks very different. Jake has floated a potential MMA fight against Nate Diaz—and surprisingly, that might be the smarter move over boxing someone like Francis Ngannou. Why? Risk, skillset, and what actually matters at this stage of Jake’s career. Chael dives into the realities of MMA vs boxing, what Jake’s background actually is, and why he’s one of the only fighters in the world who can realistically jump between sports and still make it compelling. The big question isn’t whether Jake Paul is great at MMA… it’s whether we’re underestimating him again. Because we’ve done that before. #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #JakePaul #NateDiaz #FrancisNgannou 0:00 “Jake Paul Sucks at MMA…” 0:13 Are You Sure About That? 0:29 The Boxing Comparison 0:39 Jake Calls Out Nate Diaz (MMA) 1:00 MMA vs Boxing Options 1:27 Why Only Jake Can Do This 2:03 Jake “Applying” for Fights 2:27 Why Francis Fight Isn’t Ideal 2:42 Jake Has Already Proven Himself 3:05 Now It’s About Competition 3:18 Could Jake Survive in MMA? 3:52 Jake’s Background in Wrestling & BJJ 4:08 The Real Risk Difference 4:42 Submission vs Knockout Danger 4:55 Why This Is So Compelling 5:17 Nate Diaz’s Two Options Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: This Jon Jones Story Is Scary #ufc
articles.description: Jon Jones is one of the greatest fighters of all time—but moments like this raise serious concerns beyond the octagon. In this clip, a tense and potentially dangerous situation is broken down, involving Jones and a confrontation that could have escalated fast. When emotions run high and uncertainty enters the picture, things can spiral quickly—and that’s exactly what makes this moment so unsettling. The discussion isn’t just about what happened, but what could have happened under slightly different circumstances. Jon Jones has always walked the line between brilliance and controversy. But this situation highlights a deeper issue—accountability, control, and the risks that come when those two things slip. In a sport built on discipline, moments like this stand out for all the wrong reasons. This isn’t about speculation—it’s about recognizing how quickly situations can turn and why leadership, structure, and responsibility matter at the highest level of MMA. Subscribe for more MMA analysis, real talk, and breakdowns of the biggest stories in the fight game. #JonJones #UFC #MMA #FightNews #MMANews #CombatSports

articles.title: Are You Bored With MMA Right Now?
articles.description: Are you bored with MMA right now? It’s a question most fans won’t say out loud—but Chael Sonnen is asking it directly. With title fights happening, big names still active, and events rolling every week… why does it feel like something is missing? Chael breaks down a recent light heavyweight title situation and exposes the real issue: it’s not the fights—it’s the meaning behind them. When fighters can’t clearly explain why a fight matters, fans start to feel it. From vacant titles to unclear stakes, this isn’t about talent or effort—it’s about storytelling, urgency, and what actually gives a fight significance. MMA has always had peaks and valleys. The question is… are we in one right now? And more importantly—do the fighters even realize it? #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #UFCDebate #MMAFans #FightTalk 0:00 Are You Bored With MMA?0:16 Chael’s Honest Answer0:28 The Light Heavyweight Title Fight0:51 Why This Fight Feels Off1:06 Fighters Can’t Explain Why It Matters1:54 The Vacant Title Problem2:47 Should This Even Be Undisputed?3:38 The Real Issue: No Stakes4:00 Why Fans Feel ItBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Money Moicano Trashes UFC Apex Event
articles.description: Renato “Money” Moicano just said something fighters aren’t supposed to say out loud—and it might be the most honest take in the sport right now. After being booked on an Apex card, Moicano called his own fight “S***”… not because of the opponent, but because it doesn’t help his YouTube channel. And according to Chael Sonnen, that might actually be brilliant. This isn’t just about one fight—it’s about how fighters are starting to think differently. Exposure, audience growth, and personal platforms are becoming just as important as rankings, belts, and main events. Chael breaks down why Moicano is onto something, why calling out Paddy Pimblett makes more sense than “what’s logical,” and why the old system of matchmaking might not matter the way it used to. In today’s fight game, the biggest fights aren’t always about titles… they’re about attention. #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #Moicano #PaddyPimblett #UFCApex 0:00 Moicano Calls His Fight “S***” 0:17 Why He Said It 0:39 The Wrestling Story Analogy 1:20 What Makes a Fight “Big”? 1:57 Moicano’s Real Metric: YouTube 2:39 Why He Wants Paddy Pimblett 3:06 How Big Fights Actually Get Made 3:46 Moicano Is a Bigger Deal Than You Think 4:11 He Had the Right Idea… Wrong Timing 4:42 Why Calling Shots Matters 5:08 The Truth About Belts vs Grudges 5:45 “What Makes Sense” Is a Trap 6:34 What Moicano Really Wants 7:01 Why Fighters Should Call Their Shot Early Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Conor’s Opponent? Pick Anybody #ufc
articles.description: Conor McGregor doesn’t need the perfect opponent—because every fight is already massive. The real question isn’t who he fights… it’s who benefits from fighting him. In this clip, the reality of the fight business is laid out plain and simple. Whether it’s a trilogy with Nate Diaz, a long-lost blockbuster with Jorge Masvidal, or fresh matchups like Ian Machado Garry or Max Holloway—it doesn’t change the outcome. The arena sells out. The pay-per-views move. “Red Panty Night” is still very real. That’s the difference with McGregor. Most fighters need the right dance partner to create a big moment. Conor is the moment. He brings the attention, the numbers, and the spotlight—no matter who stands across from him. This isn’t just about matchmaking—it’s about understanding star power at the highest level of MMA. And whether you’re a contender or a veteran, getting that call to fight McGregor changes everything. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #ConorMcGregor #UFC #MMA #NateDiaz #JorgeMasvidal #MaxHolloway #IanGarry #FightTalk

articles.title: The Harsh Truth About Modern Fighters  #ufc
articles.description: There’s a difference between looking tough… and actually being tough. And in today’s fight game, that line is getting blurred. In this clip, a hard truth gets laid out—modern fighters are surrounded by comfort, but the expectations of toughness haven’t changed. From missed weights to last-minute pullouts, the issue isn’t always skill—it’s reliability. When fighters only compete a few times a year and still can’t make the walk, it raises serious questions about accountability in MMA. Compare that to real-world toughness—guys grinding through long days, showing up no matter what, no spotlight, no excuses. That contrast is where the frustration comes from. Fighting is supposed to demand the highest level of discipline, and when that standard slips, the entire system feels it. So what’s the fix? Simple: consequences. A forfeit clause. If you sign the contract and can’t show up, you don’t just lose the fight—you lose your spot. Rankings shouldn’t protect inconsistency. This isn’t about being harsh—it’s about restoring standards in a sport built on grit and commitment. Subscribe for more real MMA talk, fight breakdowns, and no-filter analysis. #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #UFCRankings #MMANews #CombatSports #Discipline

articles.title: Chael: The NCAA Wrestling Transfer Portal Is a Problem
articles.description: The NCAA transfer portal is supposed to give athletes more freedom—but according to Chael Sonnen, something about it just doesn’t sit right. In this breakdown, Chael explains why the system makes him nervous—not because players are wrong for using it, but because of what it might reveal underneath. Using a powerful analogy from real estate, Chael compares the transfer portal to land hitting the open market… and asks a simple question: if everything is working the way it should, why is it there in the first place? From relationships and recruiting, to money and loyalty, to what “team” even means anymore—this is a deeper look at how the portal may be changing college sports in ways people aren’t fully acknowledging. And Chael isn’t pretending to have the solution. But he is asking the question. Watch more breakdowns and stay ahead of the game. #NCAA #TransferPortal #ChaelSonnen #CollegeSports 0:00 “It Makes Me Nervous” 0:30 Why Chael Doesn’t Like It 1:10 The Team Problem 2:00 “You’re Cheering for Laundry” 2:40 The Real Estate Analogy 3:30 Why Good “Dirt” Never Hits the Market 4:30 How This Relates to the Portal 5:20 The Key Question: Why Are You There? 6:10 Relationships vs Money 7:00 The Coach Perspective 7:40 The Hard Truth Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Conor Is Bigger Than Any Opponent #ufc
articles.description: Conor McGregor doesn’t need the perfect opponent—he is the event. Whether it’s the weight class, the matchup, or the stakes… none of it changes the draw. That’s a level almost nobody in combat sports history has reached. In this clip, the “McGregor effect” is broken down and compared to legends who could sell out arenas on their name alone. Mike Tyson and Oscar De La Hoya were once considered the gold standard. Then came Floyd Mayweather Jr.—and now, McGregor belongs firmly in that conversation. Even analysts like Max Kellerman have pointed out how rare this kind of drawing power is. It’s not about rankings or even competition level—it’s about star power that transcends the sport itself. McGregor doesn’t rely on his opponent to sell the fight… he brings the entire audience with him. That’s what makes this era different. This is about influence, visibility, and understanding how to command attention on a global scale. And whether you love him or hate him, McGregor has mastered it. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, fight business insights, and bold takes you won’t hear anywhere else. #ConorMcGregor #UFC #MMA #MikeTyson #FloydMayweather #OscarDeLaHoya #FightBusiness

articles.title: If Pereira Wins… UFC Has a Problem
articles.description: What happens if Alex Pereira wins? It sounds simple—but according to Chael Sonnen, it creates one of the most complicated situations the UFC has ever had to deal with. In this breakdown, Chael explores the ripple effects of a Pereira victory at heavyweight—from the possibility of becoming a rare multi-division champion, to the uncertainty surrounding who he would even fight next. With Tom Aspinall dealing with injury and contract questions, and Jon Jones still looming over the division, the path forward isn’t clear. Does Pereira get elevated? Does he wait? Or does the UFC build toward something even bigger? Chael also breaks down the reality of Pereira’s career stage, why a return to 205 seems unlikely, and how few fights he may actually have left. This isn’t just about one fight—it’s about what happens after. Because if Pereira wins… the UFC has a real problem on its hands.
